reasoning: PKGBUILD and all build/install scripts are safe. The 'unsafe' verdict from qwen was based on a misclassified runtime file irrelevant to AUR build safety.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen incorrectly flagged a runtime language-server.js file as build-time risk, while deepseek had token limit errors on large non-build files.
coverage_issues: Both models failed to fully review large files like main.js due to token limits, but these aren't executed during build
false_positives: Qwen's 'unsafe' verdict for language-server.js: Normal application runtime behavior, not executed during makepkg
learnings: Improve file prioritization: Skip large application bundle files unless referenced in PKGBUILD/build hooks
Add size-based filtering: Auto-skip files >1MB unless critical to build
Enhance prompt clarity: Emphasize build/install execution context vs. runtime application behavior
Implement chunking: Break large file analysis into segments for token-limited models
